title: Standardization of qPCR Protocols for Animal Product Authentication
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Authentication     of     animal-derived     food     products     is     crucial     to     protect     consumers  ,   maintain     trade     integrity  ,   and     prevent     food     fraud  .   Quantitative     Polymerase     Chain     Reaction   (  qPCR  )   offers     a     rapid  ,   sensitive  ,   and     specific     method     for     detecting     animal     species   in   meat  ,   dairy  ,   and     processed     products  .   However  ,   the     lack     of     standardized     protocols     across     laboratories     results   in   inconsistent     results  ,   hindering     effective     enforcement     of     food     authenticity     regulations  .   Standardization     ensures     reproducibility  , data   comparability  ,   and     regulatory     acceptance  . By   establishing     validated     qPCR     protocols     and     national     reference     standards  ,   countries     can     strengthen     food     safety     systems  ,   improve     consumer     confidence  ,   and     support     fair     trade  .   This     policy     brief     highlights     the     urgent     need     for     harmonization     of     qPCR     methods     to     guarantee     reliable     authentication     of     animal     products     across     domestic     and     international     markets  .
